title: Notification Service Dispatch
description: Routing notifications to enabled channels (Log, Kafka/Sink Connect topic), filtering, and stop lifecycle
notify_with_offsets_flow: FOR each channel WHERE isEnabled() AND NOT isConnectChannel() → channel.send(notification)
FOR each channel WHERE isEnabled() AND isConnectChannel() → ((ConnectChannel) channel).send(notification, offsets)
channel_behaviours: LogNotificationChannel: name='log', send() → logs message '[Notification Service] {id, aggregateType, type, additionalData, timestamp}'
SinkNotificationChannel: name='sink', implements ConnectChannel, send() → builds SourceRecord and calls BlockingConsumer.accept(record), topic = config.getNotificationTopic()
important_notes: Generic class: NotificationService
Channel filtering is always by enabledChannels list — disabled channels are silently skipped at every step (init, send, close)
notify(Notification) sends to ALL enabled channels with same call signature (no offsets distinction)
notify(Notification, Offsets) uses two separate stream passes — non-ConnectChannels get plain send(), ConnectChannels get send(notification, offsets) for accurate Kafka offset embedding
stop() only closes ENABLED channels — mirrors the same isEnabled() gate used during init
Test: Log channel test verifies exact log message format; Sink channel test verifies SourceRecord key/value/topic via a BlockingConsumer callback
